title: Intensely Rich Irish Lamb Casserole
description: A rich and crumbly Irish dish featuring lamb, grapefruit juice, and a unique banana leaf vessel, this casserole is both kid-friendly and high-protein-friendly, with an al-dente finish.
instructions: In a large bowl, toss the lamb pieces with flour to coat.
In a large skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-high heat and brown the lamb pieces until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent.
Add the sliced carrots to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the grapefruit juice, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper.
Add the browned lamb, cooked onion and carrots, and grapefruit juice mixture to a large banana leaf-lined baking dish.
Dot the top of the casserole with the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter.
Cover the dish with another banana leaf and bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the casserole has an al-dente finish.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
